title: Reference to Mediation as Irregularity
text: Any reference to a mediation during any subsequent trial is an irregularity in the proceedings of the trial for the purposes of Section 657 of the Code of Civil Procedure. Any reference to a mediation during any other subsequent noncriminal proceeding is grounds for vacating or modifying the decision in that proceeding, in whole or in part, and granting a new or further hearing on all or part of the issues, if the reference materially affected the substantial rights of the party requesting relief.

structure.division_title: Evidence Affected or Excluded by Extrinsic Policies

source.editorial_note: Added by Stats. 1997, Ch. 772, Sec. 3. Effective January 1, 1998.
